title: SHARDOR Super Silent Coffee Grinder Electric, Herb Grinder, Spice Grinder, Coffee Bean Grinder, Espresso Grinder with 1 Removable Stainless Steel Bowl
description:
About this item
【Press to Start】When you press down on the lid, the machine starts grinding; releasing your hand stops the grinding. The longer you grind, the finer the result.
【Super Silent】The average noise is 63 dB, which is quieter than other coffee grinders. You can drink flavorful coffee made from freshly ground coffee beans at any time without disturbing those who are sleeping.
【Removable Stainless Steel Cup】The stainless steel cup is removable for easy filling and pouring and is dishwasher safe. it can grinds 2.5oz coffee beans, up to 10 cups of coffee.
【Multi-functional Coffee Grinder】With the upgraded stainless steel blades, this grinder makes easy work of grinding everything from coffee beans to spices and other dry ingredients, such as herbs, pepper, cinnamon, anise, flaxseed, nuts and coarse salt.
【Worry-Free Purchase】: 2-YEAR MANUFACTURER'S WARRANTY! We are so confident in our coffee grinders that our track record speaks for itself. We promise to treat you like family! Covers any damage or defects. Free Exchange or Money Back Guarantee!
reviews_5s:
The SHARDOR Adjustable Coffee Grinder (Version 2.0) is an excellent choice at its price point. It delivers solid performance, grinding coffee beans, herbs, and spices with ease. The removable stainless steel bowl makes cleanup simple and efficient. One standout feature is its quiet operation, which is perfect for early mornings. While it may lack some high-end grinder features, it works well for everyday use and offers great value. Highly recommended for budget-conscious buyers seeking reliability!Last week I bought the Shardor CG639B Coffe Grinder, and I got impressed for its amazing performance on grinding all type of seeds, specialy coffee seeds. What I like of this item, is that is a very simple item to opperate and is very silent when grinding. Will reccomend to purchase this item for ahy household will require to grind coffee beans. Thanks Shardor Team.!I didn't use this immediately after I got it, but when I starting using it, it broke after the second use. Just stopped working while I was using it.

EDIT: While the first grinder I bought broke during use, I'm going to chalk that up to it having been a lemon because after the seller reached out and offered to make things right, i discovered that this grinder is truly amazing. It's heavy duty, can get through whatever bulk herbs or coffee grinding requirements you have, and does the job fast. I'm impressed with how quickly it can get everything to a fine powdered consistency. Definitely worth it! Especially since the company stands behind it's products.This is the grinder for you. 100% not a fake review. I’ve been using the Hamilton beach one for a year and some, went through 3 lids that cracked. It was loud, had to hold the button. This one is quiet, fast, holds a great capacity and works perfectly.Play Video
After doing quite a bit of research for a small, quieter blade grinder, I found this gem. I’m more than happy with this purchase. It has 30 settings for course to fine grinding. I adjusted the grind to come out perfectly for my Cuisinart drip machine (stay away from the grind and brew machine due to cleaning hassles and only one grind setting).

The video I took shows the noise level with no coffee beans, but it’s virtually the same with beans. I like the removable container, because it’s easier to dump the grounds into my coffee maker and then clean with soap and water. With other small coffee grinders, you have to tip the entire machine upside down to get the grounds out, and cleaning is more difficult.

All the rage in grinding coffee is with a burr grinder. I’ve tried those, and they are difficult to clean and there is more waste due to coffee ground residue left in the burrs.
If you’re looking for a solid grinder at a reasonable price point, this works great!This grinder did not disappoint. You set the dial to the seconds of grinding you desire and just press the button! Then you can dial down to zero and add a few extra pulses of grinding - for that extra little bit you think you need in order to get your coffee just right :) I love this thing and you can't beat the price point for what you're getting. It's sturdy and simple and has a bigger bowl than most grinders of this type that are on the market which makes it a better choice, I think, for grinding herbs and spices as well.I used to dread using my old coffee grinder in the morning because it was loud. This is so quiet and not annoying, and works so well. My coffee tastes better because I am grinding it as fine as it should be for my mocha pot. The settings are simple. Really happy with it!I love how greet it works for the price! It can grind on its on also so that’s why I got it. You don’t have to stand there and hold the button down. You can set it to your desired texture and just end it when you’re satisfied. Definitely recommend.I purchased this in an attempt to find something that would grind coffee beans rather quietly, and I was honestly very impressed. I love the timer that a lot of other competing products didn't feature. I can lock the lid and walk away. It was definitely quieter than the coffee grinder I had previously. It is easy to clean. Definitely a worthwhile purchase and better than other competitors.No clue how durable but it’s fast and you can absolutely grind coffee to dust if you want. The design does require you to invert it to get the ground coffee out or into the cover to transport you to whatever, but of course it does.
Great value, very satisfying.
reviews_1s:
The stainless cup for beans was stuck. Took much effort to remove and finally came off. I noticed as another reviewer had that the connector was very wobbly where the cup fit into the motor housing. I went ahead and used it. It ground the beans well but the entire motor housi g I guess you'd call it came out with the cup when I removed to clean it. The company responded within one day or less but wanted my address and phone to send me a replacement. Returned through Amazon instead. I'm grateful Amazon has clear instructions about this, to not give out personal info. Amazon readily accepted my return and credited my account shortly after I dropped it off at UPS. I ORDERED THE Amazon basic instead. Cheaper and it worked perfectly.The timer started failing about 1 month in, but I lived with that. Now, it will not grind at all. No way to contact seller, so I hope they see this and offer to make it right with replacement. I'm willing to try another one given some buyers have good luck with them.I loved everything about this for the 3 months that it worked. Quiet, the options for type of grind, capacity…but then it just died. One pot per day for 3 months, 90 pots of coffee. So, if you choose it be aware, or get an extended warranty if possible.I bought this for my mom because she has arthritis, so it would be easier for her to grind spices, herbs, coffee, etc with a click of a button. However, I'm disappointed because it is actually hard to open/close the filter & the top. Even I was struggling with it! Also, even though the dial was set to 10, the residue of the seasoning got inside the grinder machine, making it hard to clean it up (I attached a photo to see what I meant).
I just wanted to write a review for those who may have hands or bone problems, this machine may not be for you.This was GREAT for a few months before it quit working. Now it will sporadically run when asked, but not consistently. The timer on the grinder is also broken, so it'll run for a minute and way overgrind your beans. Will be replacing it soon.I bought this coffee grinder a few months ago and was quite disappointed with its performance. After only the second use, it suddenly stopped working and emitted a burning smell. Initially, the grinder was effective, providing a consistent grind. However, during its second operation, it began to overheat, and the unpleasant odor was unmistakable. I double-checked the assembly and ensured it was clean and free of any obstructions. Despite this, the grinder failed to restart. The burning smell suggests a possible internal malfunction or overheating issue. Given this experience, I’m concerned about the grinder's reliability and overall quality. I would recommend exploring other options if you value durability and safety.This is not a good product. Super messy when using. Gets coffee all over. Grinds inconsistently. And then just stopped working on the second day.The bowls aren’t made to last, This thing is probably 6-10 months at best. I should have known better just by looking at the plastic parts. $40 plus tax is not worth this thing.I received may be 30 minutes ago, opened and use it excitedly. One batch of coffee grind worked well, finely grinned. Went ahead and use the second blade/bucket. Didn’t work, grinder was hot, not warm but HOT, to the touch. Didn’t feel it was safe to try again. Returning it right away.There are stickers being used to mark the lock and unlock spots. On the cups and the lid. They are already peeling off, no way they will survive a wash with water!
